blocks.title_en: Week 8 lecture: discussion of this reading
blocks.title_zh: 第 8 周讲课：本篇文献的讨论
en: Locating the text: the professor notes that Kiser is a sociologist who applies the principal-agent framework from new institutional economics to the problem of early modern tax systems. This is an interdisciplinary attempt: from contract theory (economics) to historical sociology.

The core question: what Kiser tries to explain is not tax revenue itself, but the institutional form that tax collection takes, namely state administration/hierarchy versus tax farming/market. This is a question about institutional choice. An analogy using two ideal-typical contracts:

The professor uses agricultural land contracts from development economics as an analogy to aid understanding: tax system, state administration (hierarchy), tax farming, sharecropping (an intermediate form).
Agricultural contract analogy: hiring agricultural laborers (paying a fixed wage), renting land to tenant farmers (collecting a fixed rent), sharecropping.
Core feature: the agent has no marginal incentive and needs monitoring; the agent is a residual claimant, self-incentivizing; shares proceeds proportionally.

A student also proposed the analogy of mercenaries vs. standing armies. The professor thought this was an interesting principal-agent relationship worth examining further in terms of specific contract forms, for example, "pay only if you win" vs. "pay first, then fight" would produce very different incentives.

The core factors determining institutional choice: students summarized the list of structural factors Kiser proposes (the nature of the tax type, state size, ruler autonomy, and the development of control technology). The professor pointed out that the common thread linking these factors is monitoring capacity. When the ruler can effectively monitor the agent, state administration tends to be chosen; when monitoring is difficult, tax farming tends to be chosen. Key criticisms and responses in class discussion: Criticism one (student Speaker 3): the model assumes the agent cannot pocket the difference, that is, the agent cannot report a low income to the ruler after collecting a high one. In reality, the agent is the first to see the actual amount collected, so why would he report it honestly?

The professor's response: agreed that this criticism is fair to the model itself, but it does not deal a fatal blow to Kiser's argument. Kiser's solution is that precisely because the ruler cannot observe the actual amount collected, rulers adopt an auction mechanism: a large number of potential agents bid, and the winning bidder pays a fixed sum in advance and then collects taxes on his own. This is pure tax farming, and the ruler does not need to observe the collection process at all. The professor also used the example of the alcabala tax (similar to a sales/turnover tax) in colonial Mexico to vividly illustrate the difficulty of monitoring: tax officials inspected caravans at the city gate but could not tell whether a merchant was lying (whether he was really just passing through rather than selling locally), could not verify transaction receipts, and auditors coming from elsewhere had no knowledge of local conditions. Criticism two (student Speaker 5/Tanvi): the model lacks enforcement. Monitoring is not important, but enforcement is very important: if you sign a contract stating that the other party must give you a certain amount, you must have the ability to enforce that contract. Moreover, enforcement itself also depends on monitoring: you must be able to monitor whether the tax farmer has violated the contract. It is hard to imagine a state that has perfect enforcement capacity but no monitoring capacity at all.

The professor's response: fully agreed that this is a key scope condition. If a tax farmer could simply take armed men to the market to collect taxes without paying the crown anything, the whole model would collapse. But the professor used the example of Spanish colonial Mexico to show that, in practice, it was relatively easy for the crown to enforce tax-farming contracts: when someone impersonated a tax official to collect taxes, local residents would complain to the crown, and the crown could send in garrison troops to suppress it. The monitoring threshold required for this kind of enforcement activity (someone falsely posing as a tax collector) is far lower than the monitoring threshold required to audit tax records. Therefore, enforcement is a necessary background condition, but it is not equivalent to monitoring capacity. Criticism three (student Speaker 2): wouldn't a tax official on a fixed salary just collect more tax and pocket the difference?

The professor's response: this is precisely why monitoring is needed. The ruler knows that tax bureaucrats will be tempted to pocket the difference, and this is exactly the core problem that must be solved when choosing hierarchical administration. On the assessment of methodology:

The professor thinks Kiser's method of analysis falls between qualitative comparative historical analysis and semi-quantitative analysis: there is no formal statistical test, but it implicitly carries the logic of "in the cases I have examined, most fit this argument." The professor criticized the article for lacking a systematic summary table showing which cases fit and which do not. The article was published in 1994, and its main contribution lies in interdisciplinary theoretical arbitrage: bringing contract theory from economics into historical sociology. The professor also pointed out that Kiser's treatment of alternative theories such as Weber, Williamson, and North is somewhat of a "straw man" style of criticism, especially in the discussion of Weber; but the criticism of "predictability" as an alternative explanation is forceful, because that is indeed the practitioner's perspective. On the discussion of "hybrid institutions": a student (Speaker 6) asked whether the "hybrid institutions" Kiser refers to can combine both mechanisms within the same agent. The professor clarified: the hybrid institutions Kiser refers to mean that different tax types use different institutions: indirect taxes (such as a sales/turnover tax) adopt tax farming because q is low, while direct taxes (such as land tax) adopt state administration because q is higher. These two systems operate simultaneously but independently. On the "experimentation" argument: Kiser proposes that rulers experiment back and forth between the two institutions. The professor thinks this is more of an independent argument, not directly related to the core of the principal-agent framework, and has more to do with the ruler's learning process when uncertain which institution is more efficient. On the historical evolution of indirect and direct taxes: a student (Speaker 5/Tanvi) pointed out that the proposition "indirect taxes suit tax farming" is strongly time-bound: contemporary developed states' fiscal capacity in fact relies mainly on indirect taxes (such as VAT). The professor responded that this is entirely correct, and the key lies in technological change.

The professor used the mechanism design of VAT to illustrate this: the cleverness of VAT lies in placing the burden of proof on the seller: the seller needs to collect input invoices in order to deduct tax, which aligns the incentives of every participant in the transaction in the right direction, essentially a self-enforcing mechanism design. This kind of technology was not available in the early modern period.
zh: 文献定位：老师指出 Kiser 是一位社会学家，将新制度经济学中的委托-代理框架应用于早期现代税收制度的问题。这是跨学科的尝试：从合约理论（经济学）到历史社会学。
核心问题：Kiser 试图解释的不是税收收入本身，而是税收征集采取的制度形式，即国家行政征收（state administration/hierarchy）还是包税制（tax farming/market）。这是关于制度选择的问题。两种理想型合约的类比：
老师用发展经济学中的农业土地合约来做类比，帮助理解：税收制度国家行政征收（科层制）包税制分成制（中间形态）
农业合约类比雇佣农业劳动者（付固定工资）出租土地给佃农（收固定租金）佃耕制（sharecropping）
核心特征代理人无边际激励，需要监控代理人是剩余索取者，自我激励按比例分享收益
一位学生还提出了雇佣兵 vs 常备军的类比，老师认为这是一个有趣的委托-代理关系，值得进一步考察具体的合约形式，例如"打赢了才付钱"vs"先付钱再打仗"会产生截然不同的激励。

决定制度选择的核心因素：学生归纳了 Kiser 提出的结构性因素清单（税种性质、国家规模、统治者自主性、控制技术的发展）。老师指出，这些因素的共同主线就是监控能力（monitoring）。当统治者能有效监控代理人时，倾向于选择国家行政征收；当监控困难时，倾向于包税制。课堂讨论中的关键批评与回应：批评一（学生 Speaker 3）：模型假设代理人不能私吞差额，即代理人不能在征收到高收入后只向统治者报告低收入。在现实中，第一个看到实际征收额的是代理人自己，他为什么要如实上交？

老师的回应：承认这个批评对模型本身是公平的，但对 Kiser 的论证并不构成致命打击。Kiser 的解决方案是：正因为统治者无法观察实际征收额，所以他们采用拍卖机制：让大量潜在代理人竞标，中标者预先支付固定金额，然后自行征税。这就是纯粹的包税制，统治者根本不需要观察征收过程。老师还用殖民地墨西哥的 alcabala 税（类似于商品流转税）的例子生动说明了监控的困难：税务官在城市入口检查商队，但无法判断商人是否说谎（是否真的只是路过而非在本地销售），无法核实交易凭证，审计员从外地来也无法了解本地情况。批评二（学生 Speaker 5/Tanvi）：模型中缺少执行力（enforcement）。监控不重要但执行力很重要：如果你签了合约说对方必须给你这么多，你必须有能力执行这个合约。而且执行力本身也依赖于监控：你必须能够监控到包税人是否违反了合约。很难想象一个国家拥有完美的执行力却完全没有监控能力。

老师的回应：完全同意这是一个关键的范围条件（scope condition）。如果包税人可以直接带着武装人员去市场上征税而不向王室缴纳任何费用，整个模型就崩塌了。但老师通过西班牙殖民地墨西哥的例子说明，在实践中，王室执行包税合约相对容易：当有人冒充税务人员征税时，当地居民会向王室投诉，王室可以派驻军镇压。这种执法活动所需的监控门槛（有人冒名征税）远低于审计税收报表所需的监控门槛。因此，执行力是一个必要的背景条件，但不等同于监控能力。批评三（学生 Speaker 2）：拿固定薪水的税务官员难道不会多征税然后私吞差额吗？
老师的回应：这正是为什么需要监控的原因。统治者知道税务官僚会被诱惑私吞差额，这正是他们在选择科层行政时需要解决的核心问题。关于方法论的评价：

老师认为 Kiser 的分析方法属于定性比较历史分析与半定量分析之间：没有正式的统计检验，但隐含了"在我考察的案例中，多数符合这个论点"的逻辑。老师批评说，文章缺少一个系统性的汇总表来展示哪些案例符合、哪些不符合。文章发表于 1994 年，主要贡献在于跨学科的理论套利：将经济学中的合约理论引入历史社会学。老师还指出，Kiser 对 Weber、Williamson、North 等替代理论的处理有些"稻草人"（straw man）式的批评，尤其是对 Weber 的讨论；但关于"可预测性"（predictability）作为替代解释的批评是有力的，因为这确实是实践者的视角。关于"混合制度"的讨论：一位学生（Speaker 6）问 Kiser 所说的"混合制度"是否可以在同一代理人身上结合两种机制。老师澄清：Kiser 所说的混合制度是指不同税种使用不同制度：间接税（如商品流转税）由于 q 很低而采用包税制，直接税（如土地税）由于 q 较高而采用国家行政征收。这两个系统同时运行但各自独立。关于"实验"论点：Kiser 提出统治者会在两种制度之间来回实验。老师认为这更多是一个独立的论点，与委托-代理框架的核心并不直接相关，更多关乎统治者在不确定哪种制度更高效时的学习过程。关于间接税与直接税的历史演变：一位学生（Speaker 5/Tanvi）指出，"间接税适合包税制"这个命题有很强的时间局限性：当代发达国家的财政能力恰恰主要依赖间接税（如增值税/VAT）。老师回应说这完全正确，关键在于技术变革。
老师用增值税的机制设计来说明：VAT 的巧妙之处在于它将举证责任放在卖方身上：卖方需要收集进项发票以抵扣税款，这使得每个交易参与者的激励都指向正确的方向，本质上是一种自动执行的机制设计。这种技术在早期现代是不可用的。

shared.title_en: Shared week context: the blackboard principal-agent model & cross-reading synthesis
shared.title_zh: 本周共享背景：黑板上的委托: 代理模型与跨文献综合
en: Lecture notes: Legibility, Monitoring, and Centralization Principal-Agent Model, and the tax system in state formation
I. Core of the class: the complete derivation of the Principal-Agent model

The professor built a concise principal-agent toy model on the blackboard to organize this week's discussion of all the readings. The model's core focus is the relationship between legibility (readability/discernibility) and centralization, that is, how the ruler's monitoring capacity determines the choice of tax system.
1.1 Model Setup

The model includes two roles. Principal = Ruler: chooses the institutional type {I, D}, that is, Indirect Rule (corresponding to tax farming) or Direct Rule (corresponding to state administration). Agent = Intermediary/tax official: chooses whether to exert effort e ∈ {0, 1}, where the cost of exerting effort is k > 0. The agent's effort determines tax revenue R ∈ {L, H} (low revenue or high revenue): if the agent exerts effort (e = 1), the probability of high revenue is Pr(R = H ∣ e = 1) = p ∈ (0, 1). Even if the agent exerts effort, due to external conditions (such as economic conditions, weather, etc.), the outcome may still be low revenue. This is the key information problem. If the agent does not exert effort (e = 0), the probability of high revenue is Pr(R = H ∣ e = 0) = 0. No matter how favorable external conditions are, if the agent does nothing there will be no high revenue. Monitoring technology/legibility parameter:
means: when the ruler observes low revenue (R = L), the probability of correctly inferring that the agent did not exert effort (e = 0). That is: q
Pr(e = 0 ∣ R = L) = q

Intuition behind the signal structure: if the ruler observes high revenue (R = H), there is no uncertainty: the ruler knows the agent must have exerted effort, and that external conditions were also favorable.

If the ruler observes low revenue (R = L): there is uncertainty. It could be that the agent slacked off (e = 0), or that the agent exerted effort but had bad luck (poor external conditions). The parameter q measures the ruler's ability to "see through" the agent's behavior in this ambiguous situation. q = 1/2 means the monitoring technology is equivalent to random guessing, with no informational value. q = 1 means perfect monitoring: the ruler can always accurately determine whether low revenue stems from the agent slacking off.

1.2 Contract One: Indirect Rule (I): tax farming/share contract. Under the indirect rule contract, the ruler cannot fire the agent. This corresponds to tax farming: the tax farmer pays the ruler a fixed sum in advance in exchange for the right to collect taxes, after which the ruler cannot fire him for poor performance. The ruler's only instrument is to choose a share ratio S (the portion of high revenue the agent receives) to incentivize the agent to exert effort. The agent's incentive compatibility constraint: the agent chooses e = 1 if and only if the expected benefit of exerting effort exceeds the cost: p⋅S⋅H ≥k

Here p ⋅ S ⋅ H is the agent's expected payoff from exerting effort (obtaining a share of S ⋅ H with probability p), and k is the cost of effort. The ruler's optimal choice: the ruler wants to set the share S as low as possible (to maximize his own income) while still satisfying the agent's incentive compatibility constraint. Therefore, the optimal share is: SI∗ =
k pH
This is obtained by setting the incentive compatibility constraint to equality and solving for S. The ruler's profit under indirect rule: πI = p(1 − SI∗ )H = p ⋅ H − k

Key insight: profit under indirect rule is independent of the monitoring technology q. The ruler can sign this contract "blindly" and still get the same return. This is precisely the core advantage of tax farming: in an environment where the ruler knows almost nothing about local conditions, this is their "fallback option." Extending from the share contract to pure tax farming:

The professor further explained how to derive pure tax farming from the share contract. If there is a large pool of potential agents competing for this contract, the ruler can design an auction mechanism. Each agent knows the expected payoff they could obtain through the share contract, so the maximum amount they are willing to pay in advance is pH − k (that is, the ruler's profit under the share contract). Through competitive auction, the ruler

can directly collect this fixed payment, after which the agent (the tax farmer) collects taxes on his own and keeps all the proceeds. This is pure tax farming: the ruler does not even need to observe how much tax was collected.

1.3 Contract Two: Direct Rule (D): state administration. Under the direct rule contract, the ruler can fire the agent. This corresponds to hiring a salaried tax bureaucrat. The new instrument: the threat of dismissal. If the agent is fired: he gets 0. If the agent is retained: he gets a continuation value V > 0. The ruler's strategy: if high revenue (R = H) is observed, never fire the agent (since it is certain the agent exerted effort). If low revenue (R = L) is observed, then with probability q suspect the agent of slacking off and fire him. The agent's incentive compatibility constraint under direct rule: p ⋅ S ⋅ H + qV ≥ k

Compared with indirect rule, there is an additional term qV: this is the extra incentive generated by the agent's desire to keep his position (avoid being fired). q is the precision of monitoring, and V is the continuation value. The product qV represents the "expected loss from being fired": the more precise the monitoring (higher q) and the more valuable the position (larger V), the less the agent dares to slack off. The ruler's optimal share:
SD = max {0,
k − qV
pH

The max is needed here because: if qV > k, the threat of dismissal alone is sufficient to make the agent exert effort, with no need to pay an additional share (S = 0); if qV ≤ k, a share must still be paid to make up for the insufficient incentive. The ruler's profit under direct rule (two cases): Case one: if qV > k (corner solution): πD = pH

The ruler does not need to pay any share, and receives the entire expected revenue. This occurs when monitoring is very precise (q is high) or the position is very valuable (V is large). Case two: if qV ≤ k (interior solution, the case the professor focused on): πD = pH − k + qV

Key insight: profit under direct rule is increasing in q. Unlike indirect rule, better monitoring technology directly increases the ruler's income. This is because the ruler now has "carrots and sticks": the share serves as an incentive (the carrot), and dismissal serves as a punishment (the stick). The better the monitoring, the more effective the stick, and the ruler can reduce spending on the carrot.
1.4 Institutional Choice: from Indirect Rule to Direct Rule. Comparing the ruler's profit under the two institutions (in the case qV ≤ k): Indirect rule: πI = pH − k. Direct rule: πD = pH − k + qV

For any q > 0, direct rule is strictly superior to indirect rule. But in reality, transitioning from indirect rule to direct rule involves an institutional transition cost C: this includes the resistance of tax farmers provoked by abolishing tax farming, the fixed expenditure of establishing a tax bureaucracy, and so on.
Therefore, the ruler chooses direct rule if and only if: πD − C ≥ πI
pH − k + qV − C ≥ pH − k
Simplifying, we get: qV ≥ C
Solving for the critical value of q: q∗ =
C V

**Core conclusion: there exists a threshold of monitoring technology q∗ = C/V. When q exceeds this threshold, the ruler will shift from tax farming (indirect rule) to state administration (direct rule).** This threshold depends on two factors: the higher the institutional transition cost C, the higher the threshold and the harder reform becomes; the higher the agent's continuation value V, the lower the threshold and the easier reform becomes (because the threat of dismissal is more effective).

1.5 Explanation of the "sentence you couldn't understand" in your blackboard notes. The "coef fo I→D:C" you mentioned, in context, should be understood as: the transition cost from I (indirect rule) to D (direct rule) is C. The professor used this notation on the blackboard to indicate that this step introduces the institutional transition cost parameter, used to compare the net benefits of the two institutions.
III. A Unified Analytical Framework Across the Readings
The professor used the blackboard model to establish a unified analytical framework connecting the four readings (groups): Reading Kiser (1994) Sng (2014) Stasavage (2020) Sánchez de la Sierra
's role
Core parameter determining contract choice: scale expansion dilutes monitoring capacity; bureaucratic technology increases q q
Core dependent variable: whether q is endogenous; the choice between tax farming vs. state administration; exogenous

The decline of state fiscal capacity: exogenous. From "early democracy" to hierarchical institutions: beginning to shift toward endogeneity. Mineral visibility = proxy for q. Differences in the form of taxation by armed groups: exogenous (determined by the physical characteristics of the commodity) variable.

Logic of progressive expansion: the professor explicitly stated that the course is designed to start from Kiser's narrow contract-choice problem, gradually expand to the broader question of institutional choice, and progressively incorporate important factors that Kiser omitted, including the rebellion constraint (Sng), the endogeneity of q (Stasavage), and causal identification of specific mechanisms (Sánchez de la Sierra).
IV. Index of Key Concepts
Legibility: the capacity of central power to observe, understand, and process local information from a distance. In the model, this corresponds to the parameter q.

Principal-Agent Problem: the problem arising between the principal (the ruler) and the agent (the tax official/tax farmer) due to information asymmetry and divergent interests. Incentive Compatibility Constraint: the condition ensuring the agent chooses the behavior the principal desires (exerting effort). Tax Farming: the agent pays a fixed amount in advance, obtains the right to collect taxes, and keeps all the proceeds. Corresponds to Indirect Rule. State Administration: the agent receives a fixed salary and collects taxes under the ruler's monitoring. Corresponds to Direct Rule. Continuation Value V: the value to the agent of keeping his position; it is the basis for the effectiveness of the dismissal threat under direct rule. Scope Condition: the precondition for the theory's applicability. The key scope condition for this lecture is: the state possesses at least a basic capacity for violent enforcement. Alcabala: a commodity transaction tax in colonial Spanish Mexico; the professor used this case to vividly illustrate the difficulty of monitoring in indirect tax collection.
zh: 课堂笔记整理：Legibility, Monitoring, and Centralization Principal-Agent Model 与国家形成中的税收制度
一、课堂核心：Principal-Agent 模型的完整推导
老师在黑板上构建了一个简洁的委托-代理（principal-agent）玩具模型，用以组织本周全部文献的讨论。这个模型的核心关注点是legibility（可读性/可辨识性）与 centralization（集权化）之间的关系，即统治者的监控能力如何决定税收制度的选择。
1.1 模型设定（Setup）

模型包含两个角色：委托人（Principal）= 统治者（Ruler）：选择制度类型 {I, D}，即 Indirect Rule（间接统治，对应包税制/tax farming）或 Direct Rule（直接统治，对应科层行政/state administration）。代理人（Agent）= 中间人/税收官（Intermediary）：选择是否付出努力 e ∈ {0, 1}，付出努力的成本为 k > 0。代理人的努力决定了税收收入（Revenue） R ∈ {L, H}（低收入或高收入）：若代理人努力（e = 1）：高收入的概率为 Pr(R = H ∣ e = 1) = p ∈ (0, 1)。即使代理人努力了，由于外部环境（如经济状况、天气等），仍可能只得到低收入。这是 关键的信息问题。若代理人不努力（e = 0）：高收入的概率为 Pr(R = H ∣ e = 0) = 0。即使外部条件再好，代理人不做事就不会有高收入。监控技术/可辨识性参数（Legibility/Monitoring Technology）：
的含义是：当统治者观察到低收入（R = L）时，能够推断出代理人没有努力（e = 0）的概率。即：q
Pr(e = 0 ∣ R = L) = q
信号结构的直觉：如果统治者观察到高收入（R = H）：没有不确定性，统治者知道代理人一定努力了，而且外部条件也不错。
如果统治者观察到低收入（R = L）：存在不确定性，可能是代理人偷懒（e = 0），也可能是代理人虽然努力了但运气不好（外部条件差）。参数 q 衡量统治者在这种模糊情境中"看穿"代理人行为的能力。q = 1/2 意味着监控技术等于随机猜测，毫无信息量。q = 1 意味着完美监控，统治者总能准确判断低收入是否源于代理人偷懒。

1.2 合约一：间接统治（Indirect Rule, I）：包税制/分成合约在间接统治合约下，统治者不能解雇代理人。这对应于包税制（tax farming）：包税人预先向统治者支付一笔固定款项，获得征税权，之后统治者无法因其表现不佳而解雇他。统治者的工具仅限于选择一个分成比例 S （代理人从高收入中获得的份额），以激励代理人付出努力。代理人的激励相容约束（Incentive Compatibility Constraint）：代理人选择 e = 1 当且仅当付出努力的期望收益大于成本：p⋅S⋅H ≥k
其中 p ⋅ S ⋅ H 是代理人努力时的期望报酬（以概率 p 获得 S ⋅ H 的分成），k 是努力成本。统治者的最优选择：统治者希望将分成 S 设得尽可能低（以最大化自身收入），同时仍然满足代理人的激励相容约束。因此，最优分成为：SI∗ =
k pH
这是通过令激励相容约束取等号并解出 S 得到的。统治者在间接统治下的利润：πI = p(1 − SI∗ )H = p ⋅ H − k
关键洞察：间接统治下的利润与监控技术 q 无关。 统治者"闭着眼睛"签这个合约也能得到同样的回报。这正是包税制的核心优势：在统治者对地方情况几乎一无所知的环境下，这是他们的"兜底选项"（fallback option）。从分成合约到纯粹包税制的推广：
老师进一步解释了如何从分成合约推导出纯粹的包税制。如果存在大量潜在代理人竞争这个合约，统治者可以设计一个拍卖机制（auction）。每个代理人都知道他们可以通过分成合约获得的期望收益，因此他们愿意预先支付的最高金额就是 pH − k（即统治者在分成合约下的利润）。通过竞争性拍卖，统治
者可以直接收取这笔固定款项，之后代理人（包税人）自行征税并保留全部收益。这就是纯粹的包税制：统治者甚至不需要观察征收了多少税。

1.3 合约二：直接统治（Direct Rule, D）：科层行政在直接统治合约下，统治者可以解雇代理人。这对应于雇佣拿薪水的税务官僚。新增的工具：解雇威胁若代理人被解雇：获得 0若代理人被保留：获得一个续约价值（continuation value） V > 0统治者的策略：若观察到高收入（R = H ），永远不解雇代理人（因为确定代理人努力了）。若观察到低收入（R = L），则以概率 q 怀疑代理人偷懒并解雇之。代理人在直接统治下的激励相容约束：p ⋅ S ⋅ H + qV ≥ k
与间接统治相比，多了 qV 项：这是代理人为了保住职位（避免被解雇）而产生的额外激励。q 是监控精度，V 是续约价值。乘积 qV 代表"被解雇的期望损失"：监控越精准（q 越高）、职位越有价值（V 越大），代理人就越不敢偷懒。统治者的最优分成：
SD = max {0,
k − qV
pH
这里需要取 max 是因为：如果 qV > k，则仅凭解雇威胁就足以让代理人努力，无需额外支付分成（S = 0）；如果 qV ≤ k，则仍需支付一定分成来弥补不足的激励。统治者在直接统治下的利润（分两种情况）：情况一：若 qV > k（角点解）：πD = pH
统治者不需要支付任何分成，获得全部期望收入。这发生在监控非常精准（q 很高）或职位价值非常高（V 很大）的情况下。情况二：若 qV ≤ k（内点解，老师重点讨论的情况）：πD = pH − k + qV
关键洞察：直接统治下的利润随 q 递增。 与间接统治不同，更好的监控技术能直接提高统治者的收入。因为统治者现在拥有"胡萝卜加大棒"（carrots and sticks）：分成作为激励（胡萝卜）、解雇作为惩罚（大棒）。监控越好，大棒越有效，统治者就可以减少胡萝卜的支出。
1.4 制度选择：从间接统治到直接统治比较两种制度下的统治者利润（在 qV ≤ k 的情况下）：间接统治：πI = pH − k直接统治：πD = pH − k + qV
对于任何 q > 0，直接统治都严格优于间接统治。但在现实中，从间接统治转向直接统治存在 制度转换成本 C ：包括废除包税制引发的包税人反抗、建立税务官僚机构的固定开支等。
因此，统治者选择直接统治当且仅当：πD − C ≥ πI
pH − k + qV − C ≥ pH − k
化简得到：qV ≥ C
求解关于 q 的临界值：q∗ =
C V
**核心结论：存在一个监控技术的阈值 q∗ = C/V ，当 q 超过这个阈值时，统治者会从包税制（间接统治）转向科层行政（直接统治）。** 这个阈值取决于两个因素：制度转换成本 C 越高，阈值越高，改革越难；代理人的续约价值 V 越高，阈值越低，改革越容易（因为解雇威胁更有效）。
1.5 你的板书笔记中"看不懂的一句"的解释你提到的"coef fo I→D:C"，结合上下文，应该理解为：从 I（间接统治）转向 D（直接统治）的转换成本为 C。老师在板书中用这个标注来表示这一步引入了制度转换成本参数，用以比较两种制度的净收益。
三、跨文献的统一分析框架
老师通过板书模型建立了一个统一的分析框架，将四篇（组）文献串联起来：文献Kiser (1994) Sng (2014) Stasavage (2020) Sánchez de la Sierra
的角色
决定合约选择的核心参数规模扩大稀释监控能力官僚技术提高 q q
核心被解释变量q 是否内生包税制 vs 科层行政的选择 外生
国家财政能力的衰落外生从"早期民主"到等级制度的 开始走向内生化转变矿产可见度 = q 的代理 武装团体征税形式的差异 外生（由商品物理特性决变量定）
渐进扩展的逻辑：老师明确表示，课堂的设计是从 Kiser 这个狭窄的合约选择问题出发，逐步扩展到更广泛的制度选择问题，并逐步纳入 Kiser 遗漏的重要因素，包括叛乱约束（Sng）、q 的内生化（Stasavage）、以及对具体机制的因果识别（Sánchez de la Sierra）。
四、关键概念索引
Legibility（可辨识性）：中央权力从远方观察、理解和处理地方信息的能力。在模型中对应参数q。

Principal-Agent Problem（委托-代理问题）：委托人（统治者）和代理人（税务官/包税人）之间因信息不对称和利益分歧而产生的问题。Incentive Compatibility Constraint（激励相容约束）：确保代理人选择委托人期望的行为（付出努力）的条件。Tax Farming（包税制）：代理人预先支付固定金额，获得征税权并保留全部收益。对应间接统治（Indirect Rule）。State Administration（国家行政征收）：代理人领取固定薪水，在统治者监控下征税。对应直接统治（Direct Rule）。Continuation Value（续约价值）V ：代理人保住职位的价值，是直接统治下解雇威胁产生效果的基础。Scope Condition（范围条件）：理论适用的前提条件。本课的关键范围条件是：国家至少具有基本的暴力执行能力（enforcement）。Alcabala（阿卡巴拉税）：西班牙殖民地墨西哥的一种商品流转税，老师用此案例生动说明了间接税征收中监控的困难。

blocks.title_en: Week 8 lecture: discussion of this reading
blocks.title_zh: 第 8 周讲课：本篇文献的讨论
en: Core question: why did China experience economic and state decline in the nineteenth century after relative prosperity in the eighteenth century? During this period, state institutions appeared very stable; the only thing that changed was the growth of population and economic scale. Difference from Kiser: this article is not about the question of contract choice (not a choice between tax farming and bureaucracy), but about the ruler's choice of tax rate and number of administrative jurisdictions given a bureaucratic system, and how the expansion of scale led to state decline by weakening monitoring capacity.

New constraint added to the model: unlike Kiser, Sng's model introduces a rebellion threshold (rebellion threshold). A question entirely absent from Kiser's framework is: tax farmers have an incentive to maximize tax collection, but would they push the people to the brink of re
bellion? Sng's model takes this constraint into account: total tax burden (the official tax rate plus the bureaucracy's additional extraction ϵ) cannot exceed the rebellion threshold.

How the model produces an inverted-U relationship: a student (Speaker 3) correctly pointed out that the key lies in decomposing total revenue Y into the number of jurisdictions n multiplied by per capita income, and then plugging this into a concave function (concave function). The teacher explicitly pointed out that this is a modeling “sleight of hand” (sleight of hand): decomposing revenue into two components and putting it into a concave function is a completely arbitrary assumption, meant to produce the inverted-U pattern that the researcher wants to “rationalize” (rationalize). The model is not robust to alternative specifications of the payoff function.
The teacher's point-by-point critique of the model's assumptions:
1. The rebellion threshold is deterministic and observed by everyone: this simplifies rebellion (a complex collective action coordination problem) into a simple threshold, which is overly simplistic.
2. Tax farmers bear no consequences from rebellion: not only are tax farmers unaffected by rebellion, they keep collecting taxes, which is a “convenient but questionable” assumption.

3. The punishment parameter x is exogenous: x is the degree to which the state punishes officials who violate the rules. But this should be a choice variable for the state: the state could simply set x very high to solve the problem. Once x is allowed to be endogenous, the entire model collapses. Induction vs. deduction: the teacher believes that although this article reads like deductive research (theory first, then testing), it is actually closer to induction: the author already knew the historical pattern of China's nineteenth-century decline, and then built a model to “rationalize” this pattern. The article lacks clear, theory-derived “non-obvious observable implications” to distinguish it from alternative explanations. A student's question about the necessity of the model (Speaker 2): why write a model at all? Why not just show the data and say “this is just how China is”?
The teacher's systematic response: two uses of formal models:

1. Internal consistency check: a model forces you to make your assumptions explicit and avoid logical contradictions. Although philosophers and legal scholars can also maintain the internal consistency of an argument through other means, a model is an effective linguistic tool.

2. Discovering non-obvious observable implications: if you believe the model's assumptions and core mechanism, the model can lead you to discover empirical predictions you would not have anticipated in advance, predictions that can be used to distinguish your theory from alternative theories. The teacher candidly said that this is the use of models he values more, and also what he believes Sng's article should have done but did not.

3. Disciplinary sociology: the teacher also frankly admitted that some articles use formal models to “obscure” (obfuscate) weaknesses in empirical analysis. In disciplinary reality, an article that includes both a formal model and empirical analysis will attract both theoretical reviewers and empirical reviewers, whose expectations often conflict with each other, leading the model to eventually be relegated to an appendix or even removed. But even so, the teacher advised students to try to formalize their own ideas early in their research, even if they do not intend to use a model in the article; even writing your argument down in a single sentence has value. Criticism from students who are experts in Chinese history (Speaker 7 and Speaker 4): Speaker 7: the model has difficulty explaining the late Qing's behavior when facing enormous external threats such as the British and French invasions. The model assumes the ruler is mainly concerned with the internal threat of rebellion, but historically Beijing had already been occupied by the Anglo-French allied forces, so why did the ruler still not raise tax rates in response to the external threat?

Speaker 7 further added: the Qing dynasty's low tax rate is a major debate in the field of Chinese history. Besides the capacity constraint (capacity constraint), there is also an ideological constraint: Confucian thought held that tax rates should not be high. Persisting with low tax rates even when facing external threats might be better explained by ideology. Speaker 4 provided deeper historical detail: the historian Qin Hui (秦晖) pointed out that traditional Chinese political thought had no concept of “taxation as a social contract”: taxation was a one-way transfer from the people to officials, and raising the tax rate would not bring better public services. The Qing economy had already shifted from agriculture to commerce, but the tax base remained fixed on land. Changing the tax structure would have required re-surveying and reclassifying land, and the court lacked both the resources and the will to do this. After the Opium War, Qing tax revenue grew sharply precisely because of the customs system established by a foreigner, Robert Hart (赫德): an institutional innovation that broke through the ceiling of the traditional land tax system. This shows that the key is not scale but institutional design. Simplifying the problem to “the ruler is the principal, officials are the agents” is overly simplistic: in reality there is a multi-layered principal-agent relationship (clerks and runners were the “agents” directly facing the people, while officials dispatched by the court were actually the “principals” of the intermediate layer).

The teacher's summary evaluation: the article does capture a core principal-agent relationship, but leaves many unanswered questions: is this the most effective type of tax? Is this the most important agency relationship? Why is the external threat ignored? Moreover, throughout the entire discussion, the monitoring parameter q simply falls from the sky, which leads into the topic of the next two pieces of literature.
zh: 核心问题：为什么中国在经历了18世纪的相对繁荣后，在19世纪发生了经济和国家的衰落？在这段时间里，国家制度看似非常稳定，唯一变化的是人口和经济规模的增长。与 Kiser 的区别：这篇文章不是关于合约选择的问题（不是在包税制和科层制之间选择），而是关于在给定科层制度下，统治者对税率和行政辖区数量的选择，以及规模扩大如何通过削弱监控能力导致国家衰落。
模型中增加的新约束：与 Kiser 不同，Sng 的模型引入了一个叛乱门槛（rebellion threshold）。在Kiser 的框架中完全缺失的一个问题是：包税人获得了最大化征税的激励，但他们会不会把人民逼到叛
乱的边缘？Sng 的模型将这一约束纳入考量：总税负（官方税率加上官僚的额外榨取 ϵ）不能超过叛乱门槛。

模型如何产生倒U型关系：学生（Speaker 3）正确指出，关键在于将总收入 Y 分解为辖区数 n 乘以人均收入，然后将其代入一个凹函数（concave function）。老师明确指出这是一个建模上的"巧手"（sleight of hand）：将收入分解为两个分量并放入凹函数是一个完全任意的假设，目的是为了产生研究者想要"合理化"（rationalize）的倒U型模式。模型对支付函数的替代写法不具有稳健性。
老师对模型假设的逐一批评：
1. 叛乱门槛是确定性的且被所有人观察到：这将叛乱（一个复杂的集体行动协调问题）简化为了一个简单的阈值，过于简单化。
2. 包税人在叛乱中不承担任何后果：包税人不仅不受叛乱影响，还在继续征税，这是一个"方便但可疑"的假设。

3. 惩罚参数 x 是外生的：x 是国家对违规官僚的惩罚力度。但这应该是国家的选择变量：国家可以直接将 x 调到很高来解决问题。一旦允许 x 内生化，整个模型就会崩塌。归纳 vs 演绎：老师认为这篇文章虽然读起来像演绎研究（先有理论，再检验），但实际上更接近归纳：作者先知道了中国19世纪衰落的历史模式，然后构建模型来"合理化"这一模式。文章并没有明确的、从理论中衍生出的"非显而易见的可观察含义"来与替代解释区分。学生关于模型必要性的质疑（Speaker 2）：为什么要写一个模型？为什么不直接展示数据说"中国就是这样的"？
老师的系统性回应：正式模型的两种用途：
1. 内部一致性检查：模型迫使你明确假设、避免逻辑矛盾。虽然哲学家和法学家也能通过其他方式保持论证的内部一致性，但模型是一种有效的语言工具。
2. 发现非显而易见的可观察含义：如果你相信模型的假设和核心机制，模型可以引导你发现你事先想不到的经验预测，这些预测可以用来区分你的理论和替代理论。老师坦言，这是他更看重的模型用途，也是他认为 Sng 这篇文章应该但没有做到的事情。

3. 学科社会学：老师还坦率地承认，有些文章使用正式模型来"掩饰"（obfuscate）经验分析的不足。在学科现实中，同时包含正式模型和经验分析的文章会同时吸引理论审稿人和经验审稿人，两者的期望往往相互矛盾，导致模型最终被放到附录中甚至删除。但即便如此，老师建议学生在研究初期尝试形式化自己的想法，即使不打算在文章中使用模型，哪怕只是用一句话写下你的论点，都是有价值的。来自中国史专家学生的批评（Speaker 7 和 Speaker 4）：Speaker 7：模型难以解释晚清面临英法入侵等巨大外部威胁时的行为。模型假设统治者主要关注内部叛乱威胁，但历史上北京已经被英法联军占领，统治者为何仍然不提高税率应对外部威胁？

Speaker 7 进一步补充：清朝低税率是中国史学界的重大争论。除了能力约束（capacity constraint），还有意识形态约束：儒家思想认为不应高税率。面对外部威胁仍坚持低税率，可能更好地用意识形态来解释。Speaker 4 提供了更深入的历史细节：历史学家秦晖指出，传统中国政治思想中没有"税收作为社会契约"的概念：税收是单向的从民到官的转移，提高税率不会带来更好的公共服务。清代经济已经从农业转向商业，但税基仍然停留在土地上。要改变税制结构需要重新丈量土地、分类，而朝廷缺乏资源和意愿做这件事。鸦片战争后清朝税收急剧增长，恰恰是因为外国人（赫德/Robert Hart）建立的海关系统：一个制度创新突破了传统土地税制的天花板。这说明关键不在于规模，而在于制度设计。将问题简化为"统治者是委托人、官员是代理人"过于简单：实际上存在多层次的委托-代理关系（书吏和衙役才是直接面对民众的"代理人"，而被朝廷派遣的官员反而是中间层的"委托人"）。
老师的总结评价：文章确实抓住了一个核心的委托-代理关系，但留下了大量未回答的问题：这是否是最有效的税种？这是否是最重要的代理关系？外部威胁为何被忽略？而且整个讨论中，监控参数 q 都是从天上掉下来的，这就引出了下两篇文献的主题。

blocks.title_zh: 结构化讨论：12 点（补充 / 挑战）

points.title_en: Point 1: The Spatial Gradient of State Capacity: SUPPLEMENTARY
points.title_zh: 第一点：国家能力的空间梯度：补充
en: Sng's argument: China's geographic size created severe information asymmetries between the emperor in Beijing and his agents across the empire. Monitoring capacity declined with distance from the capital. Empirically, both land tax per capita and county density declined significantly with distance from Beijing, even after controlling for population density, elevation, and other factors. Every 1,000 km from Beijing was associated with a 25.9% lower land tax per capita and 25.7% fewer counties per 10,000 km² (pp. 114–118, Tables 3–4).

Supplementary observation: Qin Hui's analysis of China's “big community standard” (大共同体本位) provides the institutional foundation for why this spatial gradient was so steep. From the Qin dynasty onward, the Legalist tradition systematically dissolved small communities (clans, village associations, guilds) that could have served as alternative channels of local governance and mutual monitoring. The state extended power directly to individual subjects (“编户齐民”), with no intermediate self-governing bodies to buffer or supplement the formal bureaucracy (Qin Hui, pp. 263, 269–271). In Western Europe, lords, parishes, and guilds partially performed governance functions at the local level, reducing the monarch's reliance on a single chain of bureaucratic agents. In China, because all small communities had been suppressed, the emperor had no choice but to rely entirely on the formal bureaucratic chain. This made the monitoring problem that Sng identifies far worse than it would have been in a society with stronger local self-organization.

Further evidence: Qin Hui shows that in regions where small communities survived, the southeastern provinces of Guangdong, Zhejiang, and Jiangsu, where clan organizations were most active, resistance to collectivization in the 1950s was notably stronger than in the North China Plain, where atomized smallholders lacked any organizational basis for collective action (pp. 266–267). This confirms that the presence or absence of intermediate social organizations directly affected the state's ability to penetrate and control local society, a factor that Sng's model, focused solely on geographic distance, does not capture.
zh: Sng的观点：中国的地理规模在北京的皇帝与帝国各地代理人之间制造了严重的信息不对称。监督能力随距首都的距离递减。经验上，即使控制了人口密度、海拔等因素，人均土地税和县密度都随距北京的距离增加而显著下降。每距北京1000公里，人均土地税降低25.9%，每万平方公里的县数量减少25.7%（第114至118页，表3至4）。

补充性观察：秦晖对中国“大共同体本位”的分析，为这一空间梯度为何如此陡峭提供了制度基础。从秦朝开始，法家传统系统性地消解了小共同体（宗族、村社、行会），而这些组织本可以作为地方治理和互相监督的替代渠道。国家权力直接延伸到个体臣民（“编户齐民”），没有中间自治组织来缓冲或补充正式官僚体系（秦晖，第263、269至271页）。在西欧，领主、教区和行会在地方层面部分承担了治理功能，减少了君主对单一官僚代理人链条的依赖。在中国，由于所有小共同体被压制，皇帝别无选择只能完全依赖正式官僚链条。这使得Sng所识别的监督问题，比一个拥有更强地方自组织的社会要严重得多。
进一步证据：秦晖表明，在小共同体存活的地区，即宗族组织最活跃的东南沿海省份广东、浙江和江苏，1950年代对集体化的抵制明显强于华北平原，后者的原子化小农缺乏任何集体行动的组织基础（第266至267页）。这证实了中间社会组织的存在与否直接影响国家渗透和控制地方社会的能力，这是Sng的模型仅关注地理距离所未能捕捉的因素。

points.title_en: Point 2: “Tyranny at the Bottom” and the Sources of Peasant Suffering: SUPPLEMENTARY
points.title_zh: 第二点：“底层暴政”与农民苦难的根源：补充
en: Sng's argument: Local agents exploited their informational advantage to extort taxpayers. The equilibrium extortion rate ε* = Y/(2mX) increases with the economy's size and decreases with monitoring capacity. The ruler keeps the official tax rate τ low because the total effective burden (τ + ε) must not exceed the rebellion threshold r. Hence “high corruption and light taxation were causally linked” (p. 112).

Supplementary observation: Qin Hui's description of the “state broker” (国家经纪) role of village cadres provides a near-perfect empirical illustration of Sng's representative agent, extending the mechanism into the modern period. Post-reform administrative villages occupy an awkward position: “serving the state without being supported by the state, supported by villagers without benefiting villagers” (p. 29). Their function, in peasants' words, is to “demand grain, demand money, demand lives” on behalf of higher authorities. When such agents are “neither officials nor commoners nor clan elders, yet manage land, people, and imperial grain” (p. 302), they tend to become a “negative interest stratum” that pursues its own enrichment, precisely the agent behavior Sng's model predicts.

Further evidence: Qin Hui's observation about the land reform movement is particularly striking. During Communist land reform, “the overwhelming majority of peasants' grievances were typically directed not at landlord exploitation through rents, but at the Nationalist government's crushing taxes, conscription, and the abuses of local baojia personnel” (p. 39). This directly supports Sng's claim that the primary source of peasant suffering in Chinese history was extraction by state agents and their proxies, not private landlord-tenant exploitation. It also supports Sng's argument that peasant revolts were triggered by violations of the no-revolt constraint (τ + ε > r) rather than by private rent levels. Across all Chinese dynasties, Qin Hui argues, peasant wars were caused by “the court's abuse of corvée labor, excessive tax surcharges, severe economic controls that cut off the people's livelihoods, conflicts between strict household registration controls and waves of displaced people, or corrupt local administration” (pp. 44–45), all about the state and its agents exceeding the population's tolerance threshold.
zh: Sng的观点：地方代理人利用信息优势向纳税人勒索。均衡勒索率ε* = Y/(2mX)随经济规模增加而上升，随监督能力增强而下降。统治者将官方税率τ保持在低水平，因为总有效负担（τ + ε）不能超过叛乱门槛r。由此“高腐败与轻税负在因果上联系在一起”（第112页）。

补充性观察：秦晖对村干部“国家经纪”角色的描述，为Sng的代表性代理人提供了近乎完美的经验例证，并将这一机制延伸到现代时期。改革后行政村处于尴尬位置：“给国家办事而国家不养，由村民养活而无益于村”（第29页）。用农民的话说，其职能就是替上头“要粮要钱要命”。当这些代理人“非官非民非族长，管地管人管皇粮”（第302页）时，他们往往成为追求自身利益的“消极利益阶层”，正是Sng模型所预测的代理人行为。

进一步证据：秦晖关于土改运动的观察特别引人注目。在共产党土改中，“农民绝大部分的控诉通常并非冲着地租剥削，而是冲着国民党的苛捐杂税、拉丁派款和乡村保甲人员的为非作歹”（第39页）。这直接支持了Sng的论点：中国历史上农民苦难的主要来源是国家代理人及其代理的榨取，而非私人地主-佃农剥削。它还支持Sng的论点：农民叛乱由无反叛约束的违反（τ + ε > r）而非私人地租水平所触发。秦晖论证，在所有中国朝代中，农民战争都是由“朝廷滥用民力、横征加派、严厉的经济统制绝民生路、官府严厉的户口控制与流民潮冲突、或由于吏治腐败”所引起的（第44至45页），全都涉及国家及其代理人超越了民众忍耐阈值。

points.title_en: Point 3: Clerks Were Not Simply “Tyrants at the Bottom”: They Were a Complex, Double-Edged Institution (CHALLENGE)
points.title_zh: 第三点：胥吏并非简单的“底层暴政”：他们是一个复杂的双刃制度（挑战）
en: Sng's argument: In Sng's model, local agents are treated as pure rent-seekers who exploit information asymmetry to extort the population. The agent's only strategic choice is how much to extract (the extortion rate ε), and the only constraint on this extraction is the probability of being caught and punished by the ruler. The agent has no social ties, no local identity, no interests other than personal enrichment. This one-dimensional characterization drives the entire model's predictions about corruption, low tax rates, and fiscal decline.

Challenging observation: Historical evidence from the White Lotus movement reveals that clerks and runners were far more complex figures than Sng's model allows. They were not purely extractive agents of the state; they were embedded social actors whose local ties could make them conduits for anti-state mobilization, the exact opposite of their assigned role. During the Jia-Qing era White Lotus uprising, clerks in the Dangyang (当阳) religious network served as core organizers. They rallied co-religionists, coordinated with forces outside the city walls, and helped the rebels hold Dangyang city for five months. The very personnel who were supposed to be the state's frontline instruments for suppressing “heterodox sects” had become those sects' most effective organizers.
Further evidence: the structural logic behind clerks' dual role:
This was not accidental but structurally determined by several institutional features that Sng's model does not capture.

First, clerks occupied the position of “pivots between officials and the populace” (官民交接之枢纽) and bridges between city and countryside. Their daily work, collecting taxes, serving legal papers, conducting inspections, managing local registries, brought them into constant contact with all social strata. This gave them exactly the kind of dense, cross-cutting social network that the “big community standard” (大共同体本位) had destroyed among ordinary peasants. In Sng's model, agents are isolated utility-maximizers facing only the ruler above and taxpayers below. In reality, clerks were the most socially connected people in local society. Their occupational ties (业缘关系), kinship ties (血缘关系), and territorial ties (地缘关系) made them natural nodes for the spread of religious networks. The White Lotus teaching did not diffuse randomly; it spread along the pre-existing social channels that clerks' work had created, including, critically, the inter-yamen communication networks that connected clerks in subordinate offices with those in superior offices, allowing the movement to break through geographic boundaries and achieve long-distance diffusion.

Second, the Qing deliberately rotated provincial-level officials frequently to prevent them from building local power bases. This “rule of avoidance” (回避制度) and frequent rotation meant that appointed officials almost never had deep knowledge of their jurisdictions' specific conditions. They therefore delegated most substantive administrative work to the permanently stationed clerks. This created a severe information asymmetry, but one that ran in the opposite direction from what Sng emphasizes. In Sng's model, the information problem is between the ruler and his agents: the ruler cannot observe the agent's extortion. In reality, there was an equally severe information problem between the rotating official and the permanent clerk: the official could not observe the clerk's actual activities, loyalties, or social connections. The clerk's stability and locality gave him an information monopoly that could be used not only for extortion (as Sng predicts) but also for protecting co-religionists, sheltering dissidents, filing false reports, and actively subverting the state's control functions. Whether a clerk actually wielded de facto power depended almost entirely on the personal quality of his superior, an institutional fragility far more specific than Sng's abstract “monitoring parameter” m.

Third, the clerks' low status and miserable compensation, the very factor that Sng correctly identifies as driving extortion, also pushed them toward solidarity with the local population against the state. Deep-rooted local sentiment (乡土观念) made the state's formal mechanisms for suppressing heterodox sects, mutual responsibility systems (连坐) and informant incentives (举报), virtually unenforceable, except in cases involving personal grudges. Clerks tasked with investigating and apprehending “heterodox sect members” had enormous discretionary power in defining who counted as a sect member and who did not. This operational ambiguity explains why the White Lotus faith could endure across several dynasties of official suppression and even grow stronger under persecution: the very agents charged with eradicating it were also its protectors and propagators.

Fourth, and most importantly, some scholars have argued that the structural separation between “officials” and “clerks” (官吏分途), the very institutional feature that created this dual role, was itself a rational response to the transaction costs of centralized governance. Keeping clerks as permanent, local, low-status functionaries did create the information asymmetry and corruption that Sng describes. But it also ensured continuity of local knowledge, maintained functioning administrative routines despite frequent official rotations, and provided a flexible (if exploitative) mechanism for adapting central directives to local conditions. This system was not purely dysfunctional; it was a second-best institutional solution to the genuine problem of governing a vast territory through a thin layer of rotating generalist officials.

Implication for Sng's model: Sng treats the agent as a one-dimensional extractor. The White Lotus evidence shows that the agent was simultaneously an extractor, a local social broker, a potential rebel organizer, and an indispensable administrator. The “principal-agent problem” in late imperial China was not simply that agents stole too much; it was that the state had created agents who were deeply embedded in local society in ways that made them both necessary and uncontrollable. This is a fundamentally different kind of governance failure from the one Sng models, and it cannot be reduced to a distance-based monitoring problem. It is an institutional design problem, the specific way in which the Qing chose to structure the relationship between rotating officials and permanent clerks, that would exist regardless of the empire's geographic size.
zh: Sng的观点：在Sng的模型中，地方代理人被视为纯粹的寻租者，利用信息不对称来勒索民众。代理人唯一的策略选择是榨取多少（勒索率ε），唯一的约束是被统治者发现和惩罚的概率。代理人没有社会联系、没有地方认同、没有个人牟利之外的利益。这种一维刻画驱动了模型关于腐败、低税率和财政衰退的全部预测。
挑战性观察：白莲教运动的历史证据揭示了胥吏远比Sng模型所允许的更为复杂。他们不是纯粹的国家榨取性代理人；他们是嵌入地方社会的行动者，其地方关系可以使他们成为反国家动员的渠道，这恰恰是其被赋予角色的反面。乾嘉之际白莲教起义中，当阳教团中的胥吏作为骨干，号召同教起事、接应城外同教，帮助叛军占据当阳城达五个月。本应作为国家镇压“邪教”最前线工具的人员，反而成了这些教派最有效的组织者。
进一步证据：胥吏双重角色背后的结构逻辑：
这并非偶然，而是由Sng模型未能捕捉的几个制度特征在结构上所决定的。

第一，胥吏处于“官民交接之枢纽”和城乡桥梁的位置。他们的日常工作，即征税、送达法律文书、下乡巡查、管理地方簿册，使他们与各社会阶层保持持续接触。这赋予了他们恰恰是“大共同体本位”在普通农民中已经摧毁的那种密集的、交叉联系的社会网络。在Sng的模型中，代理人是孤立的效用最大化者，上面只面对统治者，下面只面对纳税人。在现实中，胥吏是地方社会中社会联系最广泛的人。他们的业缘关系、血缘关系和地缘关系使他们成为宗教网络传播的天然节点。白莲教的传播不是随机扩散的；它沿着胥吏工作所创造的既有社会渠道传播，关键的是，包括连接下级衙门胥吏与上级衙门胥吏的公务交往网络，使运动得以突破地域界限实现长距离扩散。

第二，清朝刻意频繁调动省级官员以防止他们建立地方势力。这种“回避制度”和频繁调动意味着任命的官员几乎不可能对其辖区的具体情况有深入了解。他们因此将大多数实质性行政工作委托给长期驻留的胥吏。这创造了一种严重的信息不对称，但方向与Sng所强调的相反。在Sng的模型中，信息问题在于统治者与其代理人之间：统治者无法观察代理人的勒索。在现实中，流动的官员与常驻的胥吏之间存在同样严重的信息问题：官员无法观察胥吏的实际活动、忠诚或社会联系。胥吏的稳定性和地方性赋予他们信息垄断，这不仅可用于勒索（如Sng所预测），还可用于保护同教信徒、庇护异见者、呈报虚假信息、以及积极颠覆国家的控制职能。胥吏是否实际掌握权力几乎完全取决于其上司的个人素质，这是一种远比Sng抽象的“监督参数”m更具体的制度脆弱性。

第三，胥吏低下的地位和微薄的报酬，正是Sng正确地将其识别为驱动勒索的因素，同时也推动他们与当地民众形成对抗国家的团结。深厚的乡土观念使国家镇压“邪教”的正式机制，即连坐和举报，几乎无法有效执行，除非涉及个人恩怨。被指派缉查和追捕“邪教”“教匪”的胥吏在界定谁算教徒、谁不算方面拥有巨大的自由裁量空间。这种操作上的模糊性解释了为什么白莲教信仰能绵延几个朝代的官方镇压而不断绝，反而在镇压下日益强盛：被赋予根除它的使命的代理人，同时也是它的保护者和传播者。

第四，也是最重要的，一些学者认为“官吏分途”，正是创造了这种双重角色的制度特征，本身是对中央集权治理交易成本的理性应对。将胥吏保持为永久的、地方的、低地位的职能人员确实创造了Sng所描述的信息不对称和腐败。但它也确保了地方知识的连续性，在官员频繁轮换的情况下维持了行政运作的常规性，并提供了一种灵活的（虽然是剥削性的）机制，使中央指令能适应地方条件。这一制度并非纯粹功能失调；它是通过一薄层流动的通才官员治理广大领土这一真实问题的次优制度解决方案。

对Sng模型的意涵：Sng将代理人视为一维的榨取者。白莲教的证据表明，代理人同时是榨取者、地方社会中介人、潜在的叛乱组织者和不可或缺的行政人员。晚期帝国中国的“委托-代理问题”不仅仅是代理人偷得太多；而是国家创造了深度嵌入地方社会的代理人，使他们既不可或缺又不可控制。这是一种与Sng所建模的根本不同的治理失败，它不能被简化为基于距离的监督问题。这是一个制度设计问题，即清朝选择如何构建流动官员与常驻胥吏之间关系的具体方式，无论帝国的地理规模如何，这一问题都会存在。

points.title_en: Point 4: The Rebellion Threshold r May Have Been Endogenously High (CHALLENGE)
points.title_zh: 第四点：叛乱门槛r可能是内生地很高（挑战）
en: Sng's argument: The ruler sets the tax rate to ensure τ + ε ≤ r, where r is the rebellion threshold. This constraint is presented as relatively tight: the ruler must constantly worry about pushing the population past the breaking point (pp. 111–112).

Challenging observation: Qin Hui's comparative analysis of collectivization in China and Russia suggests that r was endogenously much higher than Sng's model implies, precisely because the “big community standard” had destroyed the organizational capacity for collective resistance. In Russia, where autonomous village communes (mir) survived, peasants mounted fierce organized resistance to collectivization. In China, where atomized smallholders lacked organizational resources, collectivization “encountered no strong resistance and was accomplished faster than in Russia” (pp. 259–266), even though it represented a far more radical transformation. Qin Hui also documents that “heavy burdens, corrupt administration, and various disasters made peasants abandon their land and flee, even when they had land” (p. 51). The phrase “受田者与田为仇” (“those who received land became enemies of their land”) captures a situation where the rational response to state extraction was not revolt but flight, suggesting a much higher tolerance threshold for collective action than Sng assumes.

Further evidence and implication: If r was very high, then the reason for low taxes was not primarily fear of revolt (as Sng argues), but something else: institutional incapacity to collect more, or the political choice to maintain a low-cost governance model that preserved imperial power, or sheer administrative inertia rooted in “quota-ism” (原额主义). This does not invalidate Sng's model, but it suggests that the no-revolt constraint may have been less important than institutional constraints on the state's ability to expand its extractive capacity. Sng's model may be solving for the wrong binding constraint.
zh: Sng的观点：统治者设定税率以确保τ + ε ≤ r，其中r是叛乱门槛。这一约束被呈现为相对紧的：统治者必须不断担心把民众逼过承受极限（第111至112页）。

挑战性观察：秦晖对中俄集体化的比较分析表明，r内生地远高于Sng模型所暗示的，正因为“大共同体本位”摧毁了集体抵抗的组织能力。在自治村社（米尔）存续的俄国，农民对集体化进行了激烈的有组织抵制。在原子化小农缺乏组织资源的中国，集体化“虽非农民所愿，却也未遇到强烈反抗便以比俄国更快的速度实现了”（第259至266页），尽管这是一个远更激进的转型。秦晖还记载，“沉重的负担、恶劣的吏治以及种种天灾人祸使农民有地不种、弃地而逃”（第51页）。“受田者与田为仇”捕捉了这样一种状况：面对国家榨取，理性反应不是反叛而是逃亡，暗示集体行动的忍耐门槛远高于Sng所假设的。
进一步证据及其意涵：如果r非常高，那么低税收的原因主要不是对叛乱的恐惧（如Sng所论证），而是其他东西：收取更多税收的制度性无能，或者维持低成本治理模式以保持皇权的政治选择，或者根植于“原额主义”的纯粹行政惯性。这并不否定Sng的模型，但暗示无反叛约束可能不如国家扩大汲取能力的制度性约束重要。Sng的模型可能在求解错误的约束条件。

points.title_en: Point 5: The “Paradox of Development”: SUPPLEMENTARY, with a CHALLENGE on Its Cause
points.title_zh: 第五点：“发展悖论”：补充，但对其原因提出挑战
en: Sng's argument: Proposition 3 predicts that when agency costs are severe, economic expansion will initially increase but eventually depress tax revenue. The Qing's real tax revenue peaked in the early eighteenth century and then declined steadily despite continued economic growth. Revenue that could support 9.6% of the population in 1685 could support only 2.3% by 1848 (pp. 113, 119–120, Fig. 3).

Supplementary observation: This matches well-known fiscal data: from the Qing's entry through the Pass (1644) to the eve of the Opium War, revenue increased by only about 169%, while population increased by roughly 400%. The hump-shaped pattern with peak under the Qianlong emperor followed by slow decline is a widely documented fact. Brook's The Confusions of Pleasure documents a strikingly similar process in the Ming dynasty: the central government “was retreating from tax administration, leaving it to local officials to figure out how to increase revenue” (p. 102), and officials were “forced to find ways to collect money on their own, which could not be formally recorded in the county's account books.” The same dynamic of the formal fiscal system failing to keep pace with economic change is visible across multiple dynasties.

Challenge on the cause: However, Sng attributes this “paradox” to the interaction between size and agency costs, treating the economy's growth as purely extensive (Y = AN, with constant productivity A). Brook's evidence shows that the economy's growth was not merely extensive. It involved qualitative structural changes: specialization of production stages in Jiangnan textile manufacturing, where “each stage of production became an independent productive activity” (p. 220); the shift from in-kind to silver-based taxation (the Single Whip Reform); and the development of long-distance commercial networks. These structural changes altered the nature of the monitoring problem. Silver-based transactions are inherently more auditable than in-kind transactions: a shift to commercial taxation could have improved monitoring capacity m without any change in geographic distance. The fact that the Ming and Qing states failed to exploit this opportunity suggests that the “paradox” was driven not by size making reform impossible, but by institutional rigidity making reform unattempted. The economy offered new possibilities for state revenue; the state's institutional apparatus was unable or unwilling to seize them.
zh: Sng的观点：命题3预测当代理成本足够严重时，经济扩张最初将增加但最终会压低税收。清朝实际税收在十八世纪初达到峰值，此后尽管经济持续增长仍稳步下降。1685年税收能养活9.6%的人口，到1848年只能养活2.3%（第113、119至120页，图3）。

补充性观察：这与广为人知的财政数据吻合：从清朝入关（1644年）到鸦片战争前夕，岁入仅增加约169%，而人口增加了约400%。乾隆朝达到最高峰后缓慢下降的驼峰形模式是广泛记载的事实。卜正民的《纵乐的困惑》记录了明朝一个惊人相似的过程：中央政府“正退出税收的行政管理，交由地方官员去处理如何增加税收的问题”（第102页），官员们“被迫要自己去想方法征集金钱，而这又不能正式记录于县里的账簿里”。正式财政体系跟不上经济变迁的同一动态，在多个朝代都清晰可见。

对原因的挑战：然而，Sng将这一“悖论”归因于规模与代理成本的交互作用，将经济增长视为纯粹外延型的（Y = AN，生产率A不变）。卜正民的证据表明经济增长不仅仅是外延型的。它涉及质的结构变化：江南纺织业生产阶段的专业化分工，“生产的每个环节都成为独立的生产活动”（第220页）；从实物税到白银税的转变（一条鞭法）；以及长距离商业网络的发展。这些结构性变化改变了监督问题的性质。白银交易本质上比实物交易更容易审计：向商业税的转变本可在不改变地理距离的情况下改善监督能力m。明清国家未能利用这一机会表明，“悖论”不是因为规模使改革不可能，而是因为制度僵化使改革未被尝试。经济为国家收入提供了新的可能性；国家的制度机器无力或无意去抓住它们。

points.title_en: Point 6: Why the Fiscal System Did Not Budget for Actual Governance Needs: SUPPLEMENTARY, Deepening the Explanation
points.title_zh: 第六点：为什么财政体系不按实际治理需要编制预算：补充，深化解释
en: Sng's argument: A severe principal-agent problem leads to a small bureaucracy (Proposition 2). The equilibrium tax rate is too low to justify expanding the county system. In 1800, roughly 20,000 ranked officials governed over 300 million people (p. 113).

Supplementary observation, deepening the explanation: Sub-bureaucratic personnel (clerks, runners, private secretaries) who actually ran local government were deliberately kept in an informal, “off-budget” status. As Qu Tongzu's research documents, each county needed hundreds of these functionaries, but the formal budget provided for almost none of them. Runners received about 6 taels per year, far below subsistence. Their real income came from customary fees and extortion, which is the ε in Sng's model. This arrangement was not accidental. It served three functions simultaneously: (1) it saved central fiscal expenditure by shifting governance costs to local society; (2) it transferred the political risk of extraction, peasants blamed the clerks, not the emperor; (3) it kept agents in a state of permanent legal jeopardy (their income was technically illegal), giving the emperor perpetual grounds for punishment and thus maintaining asymmetric power. This was, from the emperor's perspective, not a “bad” institution but a “usable” one, until accumulated dysfunction made it unsustainable.

Further evidence: Zelin's The Magistrate's Tael shows that Yongzheng's “nourish honesty silver” (养廉银) reforms raised stipends roughly 30-fold but did not eliminate corruption, because as the economy grew, rent-seeking temptations grew proportionally. Sng cites Ni and Van's (2006) estimate that eliminating corruption in 1750 would have required a 250-fold wage increase (p. 123). But beyond this “efficiency wage” problem, Qin Hui identifies a deeper political-structural obstacle: without taxpayer rights, what he calls an “identity-based tribute” (身份性贡赋) system rather than a rights-and-obligations-based tax system, there was no institutional mechanism for a “tax-for-services” bargain (p. 25). In European parliamentary traditions, taxpayers consented to higher taxes in exchange for specific public services, creating a positive feedback loop between taxation and governance quality. In China, taxation was a unilateral obligation with no corresponding entitlement to services, so no constituency demanded that the state professionalize or expand its bureaucracy, even when doing so would have benefited the population.
zh: Sng的观点：严重的委托-代理问题导致小官僚机构（命题2）。均衡税率太低，无法证明扩展县制的合理性。1800年约20,000名有品级官员治理超过3亿人口（第113页）。

补充性观察，深化解释：实际运转地方政府的编外人员（胥吏、衙役、幕友）被刻意保持在非正式的“编外”状态。如瞿同祖的研究所记载，每个县需要数百名这类人员才能运转，但正式预算几乎没有为他们提供经费。衙役年薪约6两，远低于维生所需。他们的实际收入来自惯例收费和勒索，这就是Sng模型中的ε。这种安排并非偶然。它同时服务于三重功能：（1）将治理成本从中央财库转嫁给地方社会，节省中央财政支出；（2）转移了榨取的政治风险，农民怨恨胥吏而非皇帝；（3）使代理人处于永久的法律不安全状态（其收入在技术上是非法的），给予皇帝永久的惩罚理由，从而维持不对称的权力关系。从皇帝的视角看，这不是“坏”制度而是“可用”的制度，直到积累的功能失调使其不可持续。

进一步证据：曾小萍（Zelin）的《州县官的银两》表明雍正的“养廉银”改革将俸禄提高了约30倍，但未能消除腐败，因为随着经济增长，寻租诱惑也等比增长。Sng引用倪志伟和Van（2006）的估算，认为在1750年消除腐败需要250倍的工资增长（第123页）。但超越这一“效率工资”问题，秦晖识别了更深层的政治-结构性障碍：在没有纳税人权利的情况下，即他称之为“身份性贡赋”体制而非“权利-义务统一”的税制，不存在“以税换服务”的制度性机制（第25页）。在欧洲议会传统中，纳税人同意更高税收以换取特定公共服务，在税收与治理质量之间创造了正反馈循环。在中国，纳税是单方面义务，没有相应的获得服务的权利，因此没有利益群体要求国家专业化或扩大其官僚机构，即使这样做会让民众受益。

points.title_en: Point 7: Why Commercial Development Did Not Lead to Political Reform or Fiscal Expansion: CHALLENGE
points.title_zh: 第七点：为什么商业发展没有导向政治改革或财政扩张：挑战
en: Sng’s argument: Sng does not directly address this question. His model treats the economy as an exogenous variable and focuses on how its growth affects fiscal capacity through the principal-agent channel. He notes that China’s peaceful external environment reduced the pressure to build fiscal capacity (p. 119), but does not explore why the thriving commercial economy did not generate pressure for institutional change from within.

Challenging observation: This is a major gap in Sng’s analysis. The supplementary literature reveals several interconnected reasons why commercial growth failed to translate into political reform or fiscal modernization, none of which are captured by a “size” variable.
Further evidence: four mechanisms:

(1) Merchants were absorbed into the gentry system rather than forming an independent political class. Brook demonstrates that the social distance between merchants and gentry “was constantly being narrowed, and this narrowing strengthened rather than weakened the gentry social group” (p. 291). Merchants sought gentry status through land purchase, cultural investment, and marriage alliances. They did not seek to build alternative political institutions. Brook explicitly invokes Braudel: China developed “an extensive market economy... but this was not capitalism in the European sense” (p. 227). The key difference was the social structure within which commerce operated.

(2) State monopoly over key revenue sources created patron-client relations, not political bargaining. Merchants in the most profitable sectors (salt, tea, mining) operated under state licenses and depended on state permission for their profits. As Brook documents regarding Huizhou salt merchants, their strategy was to “find opportunities to enter officials’ cultural circles” (pp. 144–145) to reduce official predation and gain competitive advantages, the opposite of the European pattern where independent merchant wealth enabled demands for political concessions.

(3) No organizational infrastructure for collective merchant political action. Qin Hui’s “big community standard” framework explains this directly: independent social organizations were systematically suppressed. Merchants had no equivalent of European urban communes, chartered guilds, or representative assemblies. As Liang Shuming observed (quoted in Qin Hui, p. 271): “For a Chinese person, what is close at hand is the family; what is distant is all-underheaven... the middle ground suitable for cultivating group life” was missing. Without intermediate organizations, merchants could only interact with the state as individuals, never as a collective political force.

(4) Policy alternatives existed but were not adopted, indicating political, not geographic, obstacles. Brook documents that Qiu Jun’s (丘濬) Supplement to the Great Learning (1487) argued against state monopolies, for free maritime trade, and for letting merchants manage production and distribution (pp. 116–117). The Ming intellectual tradition knew what more market-friendly policies would look like. The fact that such ideas were never implemented points to the political logic of a system that would not empower social groups outside the existing hierarchy.
zh: Sng的观点： Sng没有直接讨论这个问题。他的模型将经济视为外生变量，聚焦于经济增长如何通过委托-代理渠道影响财政能力。他指出中国和平的外部环境降低了建设财政能力的压力（第119页），但没有探讨为什么繁荣的商业经济没有从内部产生制度变革的压力。
挑战性观察： 这是Sng分析的一个重大缺口。补充文献揭示了商业增长未能转化为政治改革或财政现代化的几个相互关联的原因，没有一个能被“规模”变量所捕捉。
进一步证据：四重机制：
（1）商人被吸纳进绅士体系而非形成独立政治阶层。 卜正民证明商人与绅士之间的社会距离“不断被拉近，而这种弥合的方式加强而不是削弱了士绅社会集团的力量”（第291页）。商人通过购地、文化投资和联姻追求绅士身份，而不寻求建立替代性政治制度。卜正民明确引用布罗代尔：中国发展了“一个广泛的市场经济……但这不是欧洲意义上的资本主义”（第227页）。关键差异在于商业运作于其中的社会结构。
（2）国家对关键税源的垄断创造了庇护-依附关系而非政治谈判。 最有利可图行业（盐、茶、矿业）的商人在国家许可下经营，利润依赖国家准许。正如卜正民记述的徽州盐商，他们的策略是“努力寻找机会进入官员的文化圈子”（第144至145页）以减少官府掠夺和获取竞争优势，这与欧洲模式正相反，在欧洲，独立的商人财富使他们能够要求政治让步。
（3）商人缺乏集体政治行动的组织基础设施。 秦晖的“大共同体本位”框架直接解释了这一点：独立的社会组织被系统性地压制。商人没有欧洲城市公社、拥有特许权的行会或代议机构的对等物。如梁漱溟所观察（转引自秦晖，第271页）：“中国人切己的便是身家，远大的便是天下了……得乎其中，有一适当范围，正好培养团体生活”的中间地带是缺失的。没有中间组织，商人只能以个人身份与国家互动，而永远无法作为集体政治力量。
（4）政策替代方案存在但未被采纳，表明障碍是政治性的而非地理性的。 卜正民记载丘濬的《大学衍义补》（1487年）反对国家垄断，支持自由海上贸易，主张让商人管理生产和分配（第116至117页）。明代知识传统知道更友好的市场政策长什么样子。这些思想从未被实施，指向的是一个不愿赋权于现有等级体系之外社会群体的制度的政治逻辑。

points.title_en: Point 8: “Without Foreign Invasion, the Qing Might Have Fallen Sooner”: CHALLENGE
points.title_zh: 第八点：“没有洋人入侵，清朝可能灭亡更快”：挑战
en: Sng’s argument: Sng treats the Opium War and subsequent foreign invasions primarily as external shocks that exposed the Qing state’s accumulated weakness, especially its underinvestment in military capacity due to fiscal constraints (pp. 119–123).

Challenging observation: The causal relationship may have been more complex, indeed, partially reversed. Without the new revenue sources created by forced opening (customs duties administered by the Imperial Maritime Customs Service under Robert Hart, and the likin 厘金 tax on internal commerce introduced during the Taiping Rebellion), the Qing likely could not have survived the mid-nineteenth-century internal crises. Before the Opium War, prior dynasties almost all fell to large-scale peasant revolts. The Qing was already facing the same fiscal exhaustion that had destroyed previous dynasties: per capita revenue had been declining for over a century. The foreign-managed customs service and the new commercial taxes broke through a fiscal ceiling that the traditional land-tax system could never have breached.

Further evidence: Late Qing fiscal data dramatically illustrates this point. Revenue exceeded 100 million taels in 1899, crossed 200 million in 1903, and reached nearly 300 million by 1911, an explosion of fiscal capacity unimaginable in a purely agricultural tax regime. This was concrete evidence that Chinese society was already undergoing a transformation toward a commercial-industrial economy. The critical point is: this revenue explosion occurred on the same territory with essentially the same population. Size did not change. What changed was the institutional channel of extraction: customs duties, commercial transit taxes, and eventually modern-style taxes on trade and manufacturing. If size were the binding constraint (as Sng argues), then new institutional channels should not have made such a dramatic difference.

Moreover, the Maritime Customs Service itself refutes the size thesis. It operated across the entire Chinese coastline, a vast geographic span, yet achieved levels of fiscal efficiency that the traditional bureaucracy never approached. This was not because the coastline was shorter than the interior, but because the institutional design was fundamentally different: professional staff, standardized procedures, independent auditing, and vertical management insulated from the traditional bureaucratic hierarchy.
zh: Sng的观点： Sng将鸦片战争及此后的外国入侵主要视为暴露清朝积累的弱点的外部冲击，尤其是因财政约束导致的军事投入不足（第119至123页）。
挑战性观察： 因果关系可能更复杂，实际上部分是反过来的。如果没有被迫开放所创造的新税源（赫德主持的海关总税务司所征收的关税，以及太平天国时期引入的厘金），清朝很可能熬不过十九世纪中期的内部危机。鸦片战争之前，此前各朝几乎都灭亡于大规模农民起义。清朝已经面临着摧毁此前各朝的同样的财政枯竭：人均税收已经持续下降了一个多世纪。外国人管理的海关和新的商业税收突破了传统土地税制永远无法突破的财政天花板。

进一步证据： 晚清财政数据戏剧性地说明了这一点。1899年岁入超过1亿两，1903年超过2亿两，1911年将近3亿两，这是纯农业税制下不可想象的财政能力爆发。这是中国社会正在转型为工商业社会的具体证据。关键在于：这一收入爆发发生在同一片领土上，人口本质上相同。规模没有改变。改变的是汲取的制度渠道：关税、商业过境税，以及最终的现代型贸易和制造业税收。如果规模是约束性瓶颈（如Sng所论证），那么新的制度渠道不应该造成如此戏剧性的差异。
更有甚者，海关总税务司本身就反驳了规模论。它运作于整个中国海岸线，一个巨大的地理跨度，却达到了传统官僚体系从未接近的财政效率。这不是因为海岸线比内陆短，而是因为制度设计根本不同：专业人员、标准化程序、独立审计和不受传统官僚层级干扰的垂直管理。

points.title_en: Point 9: The KMT’s Fiscal Structure: Extending the Institutional Argument Beyond the Qing
points.title_zh: 第九点：国民党的财政结构：将制度论证延伸到清朝之后
en: Sng’s argument: Sng’s analysis ends at 1850 and does not discuss subsequent fiscal developments.

Extending the analysis: The Nationalist (KMT) government’s fiscal structure provides a further test case that confirms the primacy of institutional design over geographic size. The KMT derived the majority of its revenue from customs duties and coastal salt taxes, taxes that did not require deep engagement with rural society. At its peak in 1929, customs revenue constituted 51% of total government revenue. The KMT was essentially a regime that “taxed foreigners” through import duties. Coastal salt taxes added another 10% or more. This meant the state had virtually no institutional linkage with the vast interior agricultural population: it neither taxed them significantly nor invested in them. The fiscal base depended on the volume of imports and coastal trade, not on the productivity or welfare of the rural majority.

This fiscal structure had two consequences directly relevant to Sng’s framework. First, it made the KMT structurally incapable of deep reform: customs and salt revenues were largely pledged to repay foreign debts and cover military expenditures, leaving little for productive investment or rural administration. Second, it put the KMT’s interests in direct conflict with domestic capitalists: the regime depended on imports, while domestic industry would have benefited from protection. The result was a government fiscally disconnected from the society it nominally governed, unable to mobilize resources for rural development, land reform, or grassroots administrative capacity. This was not a consequence of China’s size. It was a consequence of the state’s institutional choices about how and from whom to extract revenue.

The Communist Party, governing the same territory, chose a fundamentally different approach, penetrating rural society through party organization and extracting surplus through collective institutions, and achieved dramatically different results, confirming that the binding constraint was institutional, not geographic.
zh: Sng的观点： Sng的分析止于1850年，未讨论此后的财政发展。

延伸分析： 国民党政府的财政结构提供了一个进一步的检验案例，确认了制度设计优先于地理规模。国民党的大部分收入来自关税和沿海盐税：这些税种不需要与农村社会深度接触。1929年高峰期，关税收入占政府总收入的51%。国民党本质上是一个通过进口关税“向外国人收税”的政权。沿海盐税又占10%以上。这意味着国家与广大内陆农业人口之间几乎没有制度性联系：既不大量向他们征税，也不向他们投资。财政基础依赖进口量和沿海贸易，而非农村多数人口的生产力或福祉。

这种财政结构对Sng的框架有两个直接相关的后果。第一，它使国民党在结构上无力进行深层改革：关税和盐税收入大部分被用于偿还外债和军费开支，几乎没有余力用于生产性投资或农村行政。第二，它使国民党的利益与国内资本家直接冲突：政权依赖进口，而国内工业本将受益于保护政策。结果是一个在财政上与其名义治理的社会脱节的政府，无力动员资源用于农村发展、土地改革或基层行政能力建设。这不是中国规模的后果。这是国家关于如何以及向谁汲取收入的制度选择的后果。
共产党治理着同一片领土，选择了根本不同的方式：通过党组织渗透农村社会、通过集体制度汲取剩余，并取得了戏剧性不同的结果，确认了约束性瓶颈是制度性的，而非地理性的。

points.title_en: Point 10: The Fundamental Problem Was Institutional Obsolescence, Not Size: MAJOR CHALLENGE
points.title_zh: 第十点：根本问题是制度过时，而非规模：重大挑战
en: Sng’s argument: Size is the key independent variable. Geographic size determines the monitoring parameter m, which in turn determines corruption, tax rates, bureaucratic size, and the dynamic trajectory of fiscal capacity. The parameter m did not improve significantly during the late imperial period due to the absence of improvements in information technology (p. 122).

Major challenge: The strongest counterargument to Sng’s size thesis comes from the modern Chinese experience. The People’s Republic governs essentially the same territory with dramatically greater state capacity. This was achieved not by shrinking the territory but by fundamentally redesigning the institutional “operating system.” The Communist Party raised m through organizational innovation, not through geographic change: (1) Party cells penetrated to the village level, creating a parallel monitoring hierarchy the imperial system never had. (2) Land reform destroyed the local gentry class, Sng’s “representative agent,” and replaced it with cadres whose careers depended on the party hierarchy. (3) Collectivization and the unified purchase and sale system (统购统销) allowed the state to directly appropriate agricultural surplus without relying on local intermediaries. (4) The 1994 tax-sharing reform (分税制) restructured central-local fiscal relations, and modern information systems dramatically improved monitoring capacity. These were institutional innovations, not geographic changes.

This suggests that Sng’s model confounds two distinct variables: geographic size (which is genuinely exogenous and constant) and institutional design (which is endogenous and changeable). The late imperial state was weak not because China was large, but because its institutional “software” had become obsolete relative to the society it was governing. The Chinese bureaucratic system, though it appeared advanced and matured early, had long since ceased to be adequate to the economy’s actual form. Its officials’ skills, specialization, and knowledge were designed for a simple agrarian society, not for the complex commercial economy that had developed by the Ming-Qing period. It could persist as long as there were no external military threats; the Opium War exposed the mismatch.
Further evidence: five scholarly traditions support this institutional-obsolescence diagnosis over the size thesis:

(1) Ray Huang’s “management by numbers” (数目字管理) thesis. The traditional state could not precisely measure, record, or audit economic activity, not because of distance, but because the entire administrative system was not designed for precision management. Tax quotas were set by ancestral precedent, not by actual economic output. Financial records were so chaotic that even the Board of Revenue could not truly verify provincial reports.

(2) Iwai Shigeki’s “quota-ism” (原額主義) concept. The Ming-Qing fiscal system’s defining feature was fixing revenues and expenditures at permanent quotas, rarely adjusted once set. This guaranteed stability but eliminated all flexibility. When the economy grew, population shifted, or new governance demands appeared, the quota remained unchanged, and the state’s real fiscal capacity shrank in relative terms. This was an institutional design problem, not a distance problem.

(3) Wang Yeh-chien’s (王业键) fiscal history of the Qing. Wang documents that the Qing tax system had no income tax concept, no property assessment system, and no periodic land surveys or tax-base updates. “Yongbu jiashui” (“永不加赋,” “taxes will never be increased”) was not just a political promise but reflected an institutional reality: the state simply did not possess the administrative capacity to assess how much to increase taxes or from whom. This is a capacity deficit, not a size deficit.

(4) Philip Kuhn’s study of the 1768 “soulstealers” scare (叫魂案). Kuhn (cited by Sng, p. 116) shows that even the most energetic emperor (Qianlong), confronting a nationwide incident, could not distinguish real information from bureaucratic obfuscation. This is indeed a monitoring problem, but Kuhn’s analysis reveals it was institutional, not merely geographic: the Qing had no information-gathering apparatus independent of the bureaucracy itself. No independent statistical office, no independent auditing body: the emperor could only set bureaucrats to watch bureaucrats, and the watchers could be co-opted.

(5) The civil examination system as an elite-absorption mechanism. The examination system channeled elite ambition into the existing bureaucratic structure. Wealthy merchants’ rational strategy was to invest in examination preparation and cultural capital to gain gentry status (Brook, pp. 242, 291), not to build autonomous political organizations. This prevented the emergence of an independent bourgeoisie that might have pressured the state to modernize its fiscal and administrative systems, the trajectory that occurred in parts of Western Europe.
zh: Sng的观点： 规模是关键自变量。地理规模决定监督参数m，m进而决定腐败、税率、官僚规模和财政能力的动态轨迹。由于信息技术没有显著改善，参数m在晚期帝国时期没有明显提高（第122页）。

重大挑战： 对Sng规模论的最强反驳来自现代中国的经验。中华人民共和国治理着本质上相同的领土，但国家能力戏剧性增强。这不是通过缩小领土实现的，而是通过根本性地重新设计制度“操作系统”。共产党通过组织创新而非地理变化提高了m：（1）党支部渗透到村一级，创建了帝国制度从未有过的平行监督层级。（2）土改摧毁了地方绅士阶层（Sng的“代表性代理人”），代之以职业生涯依赖党的层级的干部。（3）集体化和统购统销制度使国家能直接占有农业剩余，不依赖地方中间人。（4）1994年分税制改革重构了中央-地方财政关系，现代信息系统戏剧性地改善了监督能力。这些是制度创新，而非地理变化。

这表明Sng的模型混淆了两个不同的变量：地理规模（真正外生且恒定）和制度设计（内生且可变）。晚期帝国国家的虚弱不是因为中国大，而是因为其制度“软件”相对于它治理的社会已经过时。中国的官僚制度虽然看起来很先进且成熟很早，但其官员的技能、专业化和知识早已不适应经济发展的实际形态。它是为简单的农业社会而设计的，而不是为明清时期已经发展起来的复杂商业经济而设计的。它之所以能一直维持，是因为没有外部军事威胁；鸦片战争暴露了这种错配。
进一步证据：五个学术传统支持制度过时的诊断而非规模论：
（1）黄仁宇的“数目字管理”说。 传统国家无法精确地测量、记录和审计经济活动，不是因为距离远，而是因为整个行政体系的设计理念就不以精确管理为目标。税额按祖制设定，而非按实际经济产出动态调整。财务记录混乱到连户部都无法真正核实各省上报的数字。
（2）岩井茂树的“原额主义”概念。 明清财政制度的根本特征是将各项收支固定为“定额”，一旦确定就极少调整。这保证了稳定性但消除了所有灵活性。当经济增长、人口转移或新治理需求出现时，定额不变，国家的实际财政能力在相对意义上萎缩。这是制度设计问题，不是距离问题。
（3）王业键的清代财政史研究。 王业键的研究表明，清代税制没有所得税概念，没有财产评估制度，没有定期的土地普查和税基更新。“永不加赋”不只是政治承诺，它反映了制度现实：国家根本不具备评估应该向谁加多少税的行政能力。这是能力缺陷，不是规模缺陷。
（4）孔飞力对1768年“叫魂”案的研究。 孔飞力（Sng引用，第116页）展示了即使是最精力充沛的皇帝（乾隆），面对一个全国性事件，也无法区分真实信息和官僚的敷衍塞责。这确实是监督问题，但孔飞力的分析揭示它是制度性的，而非仅仅地理性的：清朝没有独立于官僚体系本身的信息收集机构。没有独立的统计机构，没有独立的审计机构：皇帝只能设置官僚监督官僚，而监督者也可能被收买。
（5）科举制度作为精英吸纳机制。 科举制度将精英抱负引导进现有官僚结构。富裕商人的理性策略是投资于科举准备和文化资本以获取绅士身份（卜正民，第242、291页），而非建立自主的政治组织。这阻止了独立资产阶级的出现（独立资产阶级本可能向国家施压，要求其财政和行政体系现代化），这正是西欧部分地区曾出现的轨迹。

points.title_en: Point 11: The Broader Pattern: States Outgrow Their Institutional Capacity: SUPPLEMENTARY, Reframed
points.title_zh: 第十一点：更广泛的模式：国家超越其制度能力：补充，重新框定
en: Sng’s argument: The “paradox of development” (economic growth weakening state fiscal capacity when agency costs are severe) is specific to the principal-agent dynamics of large, centralized, weakly monitored states (pp. 108, 113, 119).

Reframing: Japanese material from Kang Hao’s study suggests that a similar pattern (the state’s responsibilities outgrowing its institutional capacity) can occur even in small polities. The Kamakura Bakufu, governing a far smaller territory, experienced a version of the same trap after the Mongol invasions: it acquired unprecedented responsibilities but “many of these matters were originally outside the Bakufu’s jurisdiction; after the Mongol invasions, the Bakufu became the outlet for all contradictions” (Kang Hao, p. 83). The result was institutional overstretch and collapse, not because Japan was large, but because institutional capacity could not keep pace with demands.

Further evidence and general principle: This suggests a reframing of Sng’s insight. The “paradox of development” is not specifically about size but about the gap between institutional capacity and governance demands. Premodern states faced rigid constraints: narrow tax base definitions (mainly land), high fixed costs of administrative expansion (adding a county meant an entire apparatus), and no credit instruments (no bonds, no viable paper money; the Ming tried and failed). Modern states can respond elastically through debt issuance, monetary policy, and adjustable tax types. The critical question is not “how big is the territory?” but “can the institutional system upgrade itself in response to new demands?” Size amplifies the gap (larger territories generate more diverse demands), but the rigidity of institutional design determines whether the gap becomes fatal. China’s size made the problem more acute; it did not create it.
zh: Sng的观点： “发展悖论”（当代理成本严重时经济增长削弱国家财政能力）是大型、中央集权、监督薄弱的国家特有的委托-代理动态（第108、113、119页）。
重新框定： 康昊研究中的日本材料表明，类似的模式（国家职责超越其制度能力）即使在小政体中也可能发生。镰仓幕府治理着远更小的领土，在蒙古入侵后经历了同一陷阱的一个版本：它获得了前所未有的职责，但“很多事情原本并不在幕府的职权范围之内，蒙古袭来以后，幕府变成了一切矛盾的出口”（康昊，第83页）。结果是制度过度扩张和崩溃，不是因为日本大，而是因为制度能力跟不上需求。

进一步证据和一般性原理： 这建议对Sng的洞见进行重新框定。“发展悖论”并非特别关于规模，而是关于制度能力与治理需求之间的差距。前现代国家面临刚性约束：狭窄的税基定义（主要是土地）、行政扩张的高固定成本（增设一县就意味着整套机构）、以及缺乏信用工具（没有债券，没有可行的纸币，明朝尝试过但失败了）。现代国家可以通过发行债务、货币政策和可调节的税种进行弹性回应。关键问题不是“领土有多大？”而是“制度体系能否自我升级以回应新的需求？”规模放大了差距（更大领土产生更多样的需求），但制度设计的僵化决定了差距是否变得致命。中国的规模使问题更加尖锐；但并非创造了它。

points.title_en: Point 12: A Fair Assessment of Sng’s Contribution
points.title_zh: 第十二点：对Sng贡献的公允评估
en: Sng’s paper remains a significant contribution. His model captures a genuine mechanism: the principal-agent problem in hierarchical bureaucracies is real, and geographic distance does exacerbate it. His empirical contribution, the spatial gradient of taxation and administration measured with original data, the reconstructed fiscal time series, is valuable. The “paradox of development” is a genuinely novel theoretical proposition. What this discussion suggests is not that Sng is wrong, but that his explanation is incomplete.

More precisely, Sng confounds two distinct variables: geographic size (genuinely exogenous and constant) and institutional design (endogenous and changeable). The late imperial Chinese state’s fiscal weakness was the joint product of a challenging geography and an institutional apparatus that had failed to evolve for centuries. The same territory can be governed with vastly different levels of effectiveness depending on the institutional “software” deployed, as the dramatic contrasts between the Qing, the KMT, and the PRC demonstrate. The traditional Chinese bureaucratic system matured early and appeared advanced, but its officials’ skills, knowledge, and organizational methods had long since ceased to match the economy’s actual complexity. It could persist without external military pressure; the Opium War exposed the mismatch. The root of dynastic decline was not that China was too large to govern, but that its governing institutions had stopped developing while its economy and society had not.
zh: Sng的论文仍然是一项重要贡献。他的模型捕捉了一个真实的机制：层级官僚体系中的委托-代理问题是真实的，地理距离确实加剧了它。他的经验贡献：用原创数据测量的税收和行政的空间梯度、重建的财政时间序列，是有价值的。“发展悖论”是一个真正新颖的理论命题。本讨论所表明的不是Sng是错误的，而是他的解释是不完整的。

更准确地说，Sng混淆了两个不同的变量：地理规模（真正外生且恒定）和制度设计（内生且可变）。晚期中华帝国国家的财政虚弱是具有挑战性的地理与几个世纪未能进化的制度机器的共同产物。同一片领土可以根据部署的制度“软件”以截然不同的效力水平来治理，正如清朝、国民党和中华人民共和国之间的戏剧性对比所展示的。中国传统的官僚制度虽然成熟很早、看起来很先进，但其官员的技能、知识和组织方式早已不再匹配经济的实际复杂性。在没有外部军事压力的情况下它可以持续存在；鸦片战争暴露了这种错配。王朝衰落的根源不是中国太大而无法治理，而是其治理制度停止了发展，而其经济和社会却没有。

shared.title_en: Shared week context: the blackboard principal-agent model & cross-reading synthesis
shared.title_zh: 本周共享背景：黑板上的委托: 代理模型与跨文献综合
en: Lecture notes: Legibility, Monitoring, and Centralization Principal-Agent Model, and the tax system in state formation
I. Core of the class: the complete derivation of the Principal-Agent model

The professor built a concise principal-agent toy model on the blackboard to organize this week's discussion of all the readings. The model's core focus is the relationship between legibility (readability/discernibility) and centralization, that is, how the ruler's monitoring capacity determines the choice of tax system.
1.1 Model Setup
